text.tspan: Distributed Training with Synchronized Gradient Descent

div.p: COMMUNICATION COMPLEXITY: • Each GPU sends: 2 × model_size • Independent of world_size • Bandwidth-optimal scaling BUCKETIZATION: • Gradients bucketed by size • Pipelined with backward pass • Reduces communication latency

SYNCHRONIZATION GUARANTEE: • All GPUs receive identical gradients • Identical optimizer state → Parameters remain synchronized SUPPORTED OPTIMIZERS: • SGD (momentum, nesterov) • Adam, AdamW • LAMB (layer-wise scaling)

AUTOMATIC MIXED PRECISION: Forward Pass: • Weights stored in fp32 (master) • Computed in fp16/bf16 • Activations in fp16/bf16 Loss Scaling: • Scale loss: L_scaled = L × scale • Backward pass with scaled gradients • Unscale before clipping: grad / scale • Check for inf/nan values Benefits: • 2× memory reduction (activations) • 2-3× speedup on Tensor Cores • Maintains fp32 numerical stability
EFFECTIVE BATCH SIZE SCALING: accumulation_steps = effective_batch / (per_gpu_batch × world_size) For each micro-batch i:

ol.li:
if (step + 1) % accumulation_steps == 0:
All-reduce averaged gradients
Optimizer step
Zero gradients Memory: Same as single batch Time: Linear with accumulation steps

div.p: DISTRIBUTED BATCH NORMALIZATION: Standard BatchNorm: μ, σ computed per-GPU batch SyncBatchNorm:

ol.li: Normalize with global statistics When to use: • Small per-GPU batch sizes • Vision models (ResNet, ViT) • When batch statistics matter Cost: Extra all-reduce per BN layer

div.p: THROUGHPUT SCALING: Ideal: Speedup = world_size Actual: Speedup = world_size × efficiency EFFICIENCY FACTORS: • Communication overhead: O(model_size) • Computation/communication ratio • Network bandwidth (InfiniBand vs Ethernet) • Gradient bucketization efficiency MEMORY PER GPU: • Model parameters: param_size • Model gradients: param_size • Optimizer state: 2× param_size (Adam) • Activations: activation_size • Total: ~4× param_size + activations TYPICAL EFFICIENCY: • 4 GPUs: ~95% • 8 GPUs: ~90% • 16 GPUs: ~85% • 32+ GPUs: Requires gradient compression
Layers: ├── Embedding ├── Transformer Blocks │ ├── Attention │ └── FFN └── Output Head
